mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, which would lead to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite a 2% increase in CPI over the past 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% signals a tightening labor market, which would lead to an increase in wages and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) above 105 signals an expansion in the production capacity of the economy, which would lead to an increase in the supply of goods and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative despite an increase in INDPRO above 105.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) and the Real Personal Consumption Expenditures (RSAFS) signals an increase in the aggregate demand of the economy, which would lead to an increase in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of increasing aggregate demand are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decrease in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 4% signals an easing monetary cycle, which would lead to a decrease in the discount rates applied to equity cash flows and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% and an increase in the Nonfarm Payroll Employment (PAYEMS) signals a tightening labor market, which would lead to an increase in wages and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decrease in the Housing Starts (HOUST) below 1200 signals a decrease in the demand for housing, which would lead to a decrease in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite a decrease in HOUST below 1200.
